name: Bioswales
bestUseCase: Capturing street runoff along curbs and corridors before it reaches storm drains.

name: Rain gardens
bestUseCase: Neighborhood-scale capture near homes, schools, and community facilities.
waterImpact: Moderate capture with strong community visibility

name: Permeable pavement
bestUseCase: Parking lots, alleys, sidewalks, and plazas with high impervious cover.
waterImpact: High source-control capture on paved surfaces

name: Tree trenches
bestUseCase: Street corridors needing stormwater capture, shade, and heat relief.
waterImpact: Moderate capture with cooling and canopy co-benefits

name: Retention ponds
bestUseCase: Parks and larger parcels where stormwater can be stored and released slowly.
waterImpact: Very high storage and slow release

name: Wetlands
bestUseCase: Low-lying ecological buffers that can hold overflow and improve water quality.
waterImpact: Very high overflow storage and habitat support

name: Smart Storage Network
bestUseCase: Dense areas where sensors and smart valves can divert sewer peaks into underground storage for slow release or reuse.
waterImpact: High peak-flow reduction through detention, diversion, and slow release
